prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of pyridine was computed using DFT with B3LYP/def2-TZVP basis set (tier standard, density-fitted in PySCF) at the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The geometry was from the supplied pyridine.xyz file (11 atoms) and was not re-optimized. Mulliken partial charges were used to compute Fukui indices for the neutral, cationic (+1), and anionic (−1) forms. The most electrophilic site is nitrogen (atom 1) with f⁺ = 0.4842, indicating high susceptibility to nucleophilic attack. The most nucleophilic site is a meta carbon (atom 4, ortho to N) with f⁻ = 0.1183, indicating preference for electrophilic aromatic substitution. Charge conservation checks passed: sum(f⁺) = 1.0000 and sum(f⁻) = 1.0000, confirming proper convergence of the charged-state calculations. The dual descriptor (electrophilicity minus nucleophilicity) was largest for the nitrogen and ortho carbons, marking the sites of greatest reactivity. Integrity status: trustworthy.</prose>
<parameter name="value">0.48422572
